requirements.evidence: The original LSTagPill implementation remains intact from iteration 001, and host validation passed detekt, compileDebugKotlin, and test after the weather-badge remediation.
The original LSFilterChip implementation remains intact from iteration 001, and the remediation did not reopen its selected/unselected color path.

The exact-once suggestion-chip instrumentation test still exists. Host review retains the prior environment note that connected-device execution was unavailable, but the weather-badge remediation did not affect that interaction path.

android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSWeatherBadge.kt now routes the label through `style.iconColor.asTextColor()`, and android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/atoms/LSText.kt gained the minimal typed `TextColor.Weather(...)` support needed to preserve LSText composition while resolving the weather foreground token family.
The weather-style resolver still exposes six distinct tint values, and the focused LSWeatherBadge unit tests passed after the remediation.

The four pill-family molecule files remain free of inline `Color(0xFF...)` literals. The original shell pipeline had a `wc -l` padding quirk, but the equivalent count check returned zero and host validation stayed clean.
The pill-semantics story registration remains intact from iteration 001; the remediation did not modify story files and the previously validated 16 story IDs still satisfy the 12+ requirement.

The exact-once filter-chip instrumentation test still exists. As with AC-3, host review retains the existing no-device environment note rather than treating it as a code defect in this remediation pass.

summary: APPROVED: iteration 002 fixes the only code defect from review by keeping LSWeatherBadge on the LSText atom path while resolving weather-specific foreground tokens via a minimal typed LSText extension. Host detekt, compile, and test validation passed, and the prior connected-device limitation remains only an environment note.
